VIEWER OF LOCAL BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED COMPLETE LOSS OF ANIMAL SHELTERS, A BARN, AND WORKING EQUIPMENT, AS WELL AS LOSS OF ROOF. ALSO REPORTED HEARING THE ROARING SOUND OF A TRAIN. THIS DAMAGE REPORT CAN BE CORRELATED WITH A REPORT FROM MARSHALL COUNTY AL EMA DIRECTOR OF TWO SIGHTINGS OF A TORNADO BY A CITIZEN TRAVELING ALONG US HWY 431 AND LOOKING NORTHEASTWARD DOWN AL HWY 79 AND NORTHEASTWARD DOWN GUNTERSVILLE LAKE FROM HOUSTON BRIDGE.

DAMAGE FROM AN EF-1 TORNADO WAS SURVEYED BETWEEN WHITE CITY AND HOLLY POND FROM A SUPERCELL THAT OCCURRED YESTERDAY AFTERNOON. IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 20 MINUTES, HAD A MAX WIND SPEED OF 105MPH, MAX WIDTH OF 112 YARDS, AND HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 11.07MI. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN BOTH WHITE CITY AND HOLLY POND, WITH SCATTERED HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED/UPROOTED BETWEEN THE TWO CITIES. A FEW FARM/OUTBUILDINGS/BARNS SUSTAINED DAMAGE BETWEEN THE TWO CITIES AS WELL. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 0.2MI SOUTHWEST OF WHITE CITY AND LIFTED JUST NORTHEAST OF CEMETERY ROAD NORTHEAST OF HOLLY POND.
DAMAGE TO THE WHITE CITY COMMUNITY CENTER. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
